notes: CI-safe scenarios are contract scaffolding until supervisor IPC lands.
Local full-runtime scenarios must use the existing Chrome extension/native-host path, not a Playwright-managed product substitute.

scenarios.setup: Seed a dead broker_endpoint_v1.json fixture before runtime.ensure_ready.
assertions: Cached pid/socket is treated as a hint only.
Live runtime.hello handshake is required before attach.
Heal/status reports the stale endpoint as pruned or ignored.

scenarios.setup: Start a CLI browser run, restart the supervisor before first side-effecting step, then retry.
assertions: CLI reattaches through rzn.local.v1 handshake.
No manual pid/socket cleanup is required.
Run resumes or fails explicitly before side effects are replayed.

scenarios.setup: Model an MCP stdio adapter exit while supervisor session state remains live.
assertions: Supervisor owns session state, not the MCP subprocess.
Recreated MCP adapter can call browser.snapshot with the same session_id.
MCP tool names remain browser.session_open, browser.snapshot, browser.execute_step, browser.poll_events, and browser.session_close.

scenarios.setup: Fake dispatcher redelivers the same command_id with a new lease_id after supervisor restart.
assertions: Supervisor dedupes by command_id before extension dispatch.
lease_id tracks the attempt but does not become the extension req_id.
payload.session_id is normalized to the cloud envelope session_id.

scenarios.setup: Restart the Chrome-launched native host while a cloud actor poll loop is active.
assertions: Supervisor cloud.status remains the actor authority.
Native host reconnects as a bridge only.
Refresh token and command spool remain supervisor-owned.

scenarios.setup: Model Reason app closing while the supervisor remains live, then recreate the app client.
assertions: Browser automation is still available to CLI, MCP, and cloud while the app is closed.
App reconnect calls runtime.ensure_ready instead of spawning a browser plugin worker.
App-owned non-browser MCP/plugin capabilities are unaffected.

scenarios.setup: Run the app browser smoke, reload the extension service worker, then run snapshot/execute_step again.
assertions: App calls continue through supervisor-backed browser tools.
Extension session metadata reloads before command handling.
No plugin worker selection is required for the default browser path.

scenarios.setup: Trigger MV3 service-worker reload during an open browser session.
assertions: Supervisor reports bridge degraded during disconnect.
Bridge reconnect updates runtime.status.
Session/tab affinity is restored or a structured NO_WORKFLOW_TAB-style error is returned.

scenarios.setup: Quit and reopen Chrome with the installed extension while supervisor remains live.
assertions: Supervisor remains alive and marks extension unavailable while Chrome is closed.
New native-host bridge registers after Chrome/extension reconnect.
No stale worker pid or socket cleanup is required.
